layman_headline: Extreme Fear grips crypto — patience beats impulse in this environment.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in 'Extreme Fear' territory at 22/100, with Bitcoin sliding below $63K and most altcoins bleeding 10-30% over the past week. The US government just moved $297M in seized crypto to Coinbase, which often spooks traders worried about sell pressure. While fear often creates buying opportunities, none of the current candidates show strong enough technical setups — most are in confirmed downtrends with bearish chart structures and poor risk/reward ratios.
layman_action: Stay in cash and wait. The market hasn't shown a clear reversal signal yet. Watch for a stabilization in Bitcoin and oversold bounces with volume confirmation before committing capital.
expert_reasoning: I evaluated all seven candidates and none meet swing trading entry criteria. BONK (RSI 33) is deeply oversold but has a -1.40 chart bias with bearish Marubozu — pure selling conviction, don't catch that knife. GRASS dropped 33.7% in a week but risk/reward is only 0.69x with a bearish chart bias. KITE shows a bullish engulfing but is in a downtrend with -20.3% 5-day momentum. MON is the closest to actionable with RSI 36.85, bullish Marubozu, and slight positive 5-day momentum, but R/R at 0.79x and all MAs bearish means the setup isn't high-quality enough. JTO has the only positive chart bias (+0.40) and is above its SMA20, but RSI at 59 is already elevated after a bounce. The US government moving $297M BTC/ETH to Coinbase Prime is a near-term overhang. BTC dominance at 56.1% continues to squeeze altcoins. I'm waiting for volume-confirmed breakouts — my strongest reliable signal at 100% win rate — before deploying capital.
risk_factors: US government $297M crypto transfer to Coinbase could trigger selling pressure across the market
BTC dominance at 56.1% and rising actively suppresses altcoin performance
All swing candidates are below key moving averages with bearish market structure — catching a falling knife risk is elevated
watch_list: MON price action around $0.0185 support — a bounce with volume here would create a proper entry
Bitcoin reaction to upcoming inflation report and whether $60K support holds
Fear & Greed index — currently improving from 23.9 weekly avg to 22 daily; a move toward 15-18 would signal capitulation bottoming
market_context: Crypto market is in Extreme Fear (22/100) with Bitcoin at $62.5K facing headwinds from Fed rate hike expectations and a $297M government crypto transfer to Coinbase. High BTC dominance at 56.1% signals capital rotation away from altcoins, making alt entries particularly risky right now.
context_banner: Extreme Fear creates opportunity, but discipline demands waiting for volume-confirmed reversals rather than catching falling knives in a bearish alt environment.

key_signals.description: Extreme Fear at 22/100 with improving trend suggests we're approaching a bottoming zone, but not yet confirmed — historically a precondition for bounces.
US government moved $297M in seized Bitcoin and Ether to Coinbase Prime, creating near-term sell pressure overhang across the market.
Bitcoin slipping as traders increase July Fed rate hike bets ahead of inflation report — macro headwind keeping risk assets under pressure.
MON (36.85) and BONK (33.09) approaching oversold territory, but neither shows volume confirmation or bullish cross to trigger entry.
No volume breakout detected on any candidate — this highest-reliability signal (100% win rate) is absent, supporting a HOLD decision.
